Brand Guidelines

The visual system behind the Griffiths, PC site — a mark, a palette grounded in real phthalo pigment, a type ramp, and a generative dithering technique — documented here so it stays consistent across the site and anything printed from it.

Logo Color Typography Dithering Imagery Applications
Logo

The Mark

A lowercase serif “g”, set in the same display face as the wordmark, in a solid roundel. Two variants: standard for light backgrounds, reversed for dark.

Large — 96px
Medium — 56px
Small — 32px (min. size)

Clearspace

Keep a margin of at least half the mark’s diameter clear on all sides — don’t crowd it against text or edges.

Color

Only the two documented variants (standard ink-on-paper, reversed paper-on-ink). Never recolor to an off-brand hue.

Minimum size

Don’t render smaller than 32px / 0.25in — the glyph loses legibility below that.

Don’t

No drop shadows, outlines, gradients, or rotation. No stretching off the 1:1 circle.

Color

Palette

Built around a real phthalo green pigment reference (#123524) rather than an arbitrary hex — the accent and ink tones are the same hue at different concentrations, the way the actual pigment behaves at different dilutions.

Accent Ink

#123524

Phthalo green mass-tone. Headlines on dark, logo fill, darkest brand value.

Accent

#1C5439

Mid-concentration phthalo green. Eyebrows, links, hover states, CTA hover.

Dither Tint

#319162

Bright phthalo tint, used only for the dithering effect so it reads as color, not gray, at small dot size.

Ink

#14181B

Primary text color. Also the CTA button fill.

Ink Soft

#4A5049

Secondary / muted text — body copy, captions, credentials.

Paper

#E7E9E3

Primary background. The site commits to this single light register — no dark mode.

Paper Deep

#DBDFD5

Secondary surface — alternating section backgrounds (About, closing blocks).

Line

#B9BFB2

Hairline borders and section dividers.

Typography

Type Ramp

Three roles: a serif display face for headlines and the wordmark, a system sans for body copy, and a monospace for labels, credentials, and anything tabular.

Display / H1
Griffiths, PC
ui-serif · clamp(2.75rem, 8vw, 6rem) on the live hero
Display / H2
Purpose-Driven, Future-Focused
ui-serif · clamp(2rem, 4.2vw, 3.1rem)
Display / H3
Electric Utility Generation Offtakes & Interconnection
ui-serif · ~1.2–1.5rem depending on context
Body

Backed by over 20 years of industry experience, our firm provides high-quality advice and creative solutions to modern problems.

System sans · 1rem base / 1.65 line-height
Mono Label
Energy Law & Infrastructure Development
ui-monospace · 0.72–0.85rem, letter-spacing 0.1–0.18em, uppercase
Mono Data
B.A. Business Economics, UC Santa Barbara · J.D., UC Davis School of Law
ui-monospace · credentials, addresses, phone numbers (tabular)
Displayui-serif, "New York", "Iowan Old Style", "Palatino Linotype", Georgia, "Times New Roman", serif
Body-apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, "SF Pro Text", system-ui, "Segoe UI", Roboto, sans-serif
Monoui-monospace, "SF Mono", Menlo, "Cascadia Code", "Roboto Mono", monospace
Dithering

The Halftone Effect

An 8×8 ordered (Bayer) dither over a smoothly-warping cosine wave field, rendered in two WebGL passes and colored in the Dither Tint. It's the site's one generative flourish — reserved for hero moments, not scattered as decoration.

Where it's used

The homepage hero background only. It commits to a fixed light register regardless of the rest of the page.

How it's built

Pass 1 renders a slow cosine-wave interference field to an offscreen texture. Pass 2 samples it through a Bayer 8×8 threshold to produce small round dots — not filled squares.

Interaction

The pattern clears in a soft radius around the cursor. Move your mouse over the demo above.

Reuse rules

Reserve for one hero moment per page. For print/static assets (business cards, banners), bake a single static frame rather than animating.

Imagery

Photography

Currently sourced from Unsplash, chosen to put a real charge behind the “energy law & infrastructure” framing — transmission lines, industrial scale, work in progress — rather than generic law-firm stock. Full color throughout; the site doesn’t desaturate or duotone photography. These are placeholders, not a locked style, so the notes below are what should carry over whenever they’re replaced with the firm’s own photography.

Hero — homepage
Practice areas — primary
Practice areas — secondary
Team — page header
Contact — page header

Full color, always

No desaturation, duotone, or grayscale treatments — full color reads as more current and more credible than a stylized filter.

Scale over staging

Wide industrial and infrastructure landscapes, not staged handshakes or gavels — the work is physical infrastructure, so the imagery should look like it.

Landscape, full-bleed

Hero and section headers run wide edge-to-edge. Vertical or tightly-cropped shots don’t hold up at that aspect ratio.

Swapping these out

Unsplash placeholders today — when firm-owned photography is available, match the same color, scale, and framing guidance above rather than introducing a new look.

Applications

Merch & Collateral

The same mark, palette, and dithering technique applied to a few real-world pieces — kept simple on purpose.
